title: Message ('M')
text: Message ('M') Translate an error code to a text. Description Provide Uniface with a string that contains the error message text corresponding to a connector error code or a DBMS error code. The Message request must be successful, if necessary, by supplying a default error message. From Uniface Available Uniface information for the Message request. Table 1. Information Available from Uniface Data Structure->Element Description uctrl->udrverr Error code to be translated. This is a code that has been supplied to Uniface by the connector itself in uctrl->udrverr . uctrl->udrvini Unique identification of the current logon path. uctrl->iobuf Points to the general I/O buffer. uctrl->sqlbuf Points to the SQL buffer. uctrl->udrvnum Connector sequence number. To Uniface The connector request return value must be 0. Do not change the value of uctrl->udrverr . The error message must be stored as a null-terminated string of printable ASCII characters in the general I/O buffer. The message length is only limited by the size of the general I/O buffer. Table 2. Connector Request Return Values Value Description 0 Success -9 I/O server logon error. Service functions Not applicable. Extra information The Message request can be provided with a dummy control block. This control block does not describe a table and it does not have an associated connector workspace. The Message request can be called without a current logon path ( uctrl->udrvini = 0 ). Related Links uctrl Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table.
